programme.title: Aliens
programme.desc: Ripley, the sole survivor of the Nostromo's deadly encounter with the monstrous Alien, returns to Earth after drifting through space in hypersleep for 57 years. Although her story is initially met with skepticism, she agrees to accompany a team of Colonial Marines back to LV-426.

programme.title: Ballerina
programme.desc: An assassin trained in the traditions of the Ruska Roma organization sets out to seek revenge after her father's death.

programme.title: Unforgiven
programme.desc: William Munny is a retired, once-ruthless killer turned gentle widower and hog farmer. To help support his two motherless children, he accepts one last bounty-hunter mission to find the men who brutalized a prostitute. Joined by his former partner and a cocky greenhorn, he takes on a corrupt sheriff.

programme.title: The League of Extraordinary Gentlemen
programme.desc: Renowned adventurer Allan Quatermain leads a team of extraordinary figures with legendary powers to battle the technological terror of a madman known as "The Fantom". This "League" comprises seafarer and inventor Captain Nemo, vampire Mina Harker, an invisible man named Rodney Skinner, American Secret Service Agent Tom Sawyer, the ageless and invincible Dorian Gray, and the dangerous split personality of Dr. Jekyll and Mr. Hyde.

programme.title: Indiana Jones and the Temple of Doom
programme.desc: After arriving in India, Indiana Jones is asked by a desperate village to find a mystical stone. He agrees – and stumbles upon a secret cult plotting a terrible plan in the catacombs of an ancient palace.

programme.title: The Chronicles of Riddick
programme.desc: The wanted criminal Richard Bruno Riddick arrives on a planet called Helion Prime and finds himself up against an invading empire called the Necromongers, an army that plans to convert or kill all humans in the universe.

programme.title: Nobody 2
programme.desc: Workaholic assassin Hutch Mansell takes his family on a much-needed vacation to the small tourist town of Plummerville. However, he soon finds himself in the crosshairs of a corrupt theme-park operator, a shady sheriff, and a bloodthirsty crime boss.

programme.title: Unforgiven
programme.desc: William Munny is a retired, once-ruthless killer turned gentle widower and hog farmer. To help support his two motherless children, he accepts one last bounty-hunter mission to find the men who brutalized a prostitute. Joined by his former partner and a cocky greenhorn, he takes on a corrupt sheriff.

programme.title: Sherlock Holmes: A Game of Shadows
programme.desc: There is a new criminal mastermind at large (Professor Moriarty) and not only is he Holmes’ intellectual equal, but his capacity for evil and lack of conscience may give him an advantage over the detective.

programme.title: Underworld
programme.desc: Vampires and werewolves have waged a nocturnal war against each other for centuries. But all bets are off when a female vampire warrior named Selene, who's famous for her strength and werewolf-hunting prowess, becomes smitten with a peace-loving male werewolf, Michael, who wants to end the war.

programme.title: Resident Evil
programme.desc: When a virus leaks from a top-secret facility, turning all resident researchers into ravenous zombies and their lab animals into mutated hounds from hell, the government sends in an elite military task force to contain the outbreak.

programme.title: Terminator 2: Judgment Day
programme.desc: Ten years after the events of the original, a reprogrammed T-800 is sent back in time to protect young John Connor from the shape-shifting T-1000. Together with his mother Sarah, he fights to stop Skynet from triggering a nuclear apocalypse.

programme.title: WCW Main Event - 5x01 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 5x02 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 5x03 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 5x04 - WCW Main Event
Home Alone 2: Lost in New York
programme.desc: Kevin is separated from his family again when he accidentally boards a flight to New York City during a Christmas trip to Miami. However he crosses paths with the same burglars, who now plan to rob a toy store on Christmas Eve.

programme.title: Caddyshack
programme.desc: At an exclusive country club, an ambitious young caddy, Danny Noonan, eagerly pursues a caddy scholarship in hopes of attending college and, in turn, avoiding a job at the lumber yard. In order to succeed, he must first win the favour of the elitist Judge Smails, and then the caddy golf tournament which Smails sponsors.

programme.title: Napoleon Dynamite
programme.desc: Preston, Idaho's most curious resident, Napoleon Dynamite, lives with his grandma and his 32-year-old brother (who cruises chat rooms for ladies) and works to help his best friend, Pedro, snatch the Student Body President title from mean teen Summer Wheatley.

programme.title: Police Academy 6: City Under Siege
programme.desc: Our favourite police men are called together to deal with a gang who rob banks and jewelers. Using their various talents as well as their extraordinary luck, the crooks stand no chance against our men and women in blue.

programme.title: Revenge of the Nerds II: Nerds in Paradise
programme.desc: Get out your white socks! This time our suave college heroes hit the road for fun and fraternity in Florida. Another opportunity for brains to triumph over brawn. How can the girls possibly resist their geeky charms?

programme.title: Pee-wee's Big Adventure
programme.desc: The cartoonish and childish character Pee-wee Herman goes on a big adventure for the first time ever when his beloved shiny new bicycle is stolen by his nemesis Francis Buxton, a fellow man-child and neighborhood rich "kid." And he sets off on an obsessive cross-country journey, determined to recover it. Pee-wee's awkward and childish attempts to be cool and mature.

programme.title: Beetlejuice Beetlejuice
programme.desc: After a family tragedy, three generations of the Deetz family return home to Winter River. Still haunted by Beetlejuice, Lydia's life is turned upside down when her teenage daughter, Astrid, accidentally opens the portal to the Afterlife.

programme.title: Revenge of the Nerds
programme.desc: At Adams College, the jocks rule the school from their house on high, the Alpha Beta fraternity. So when a group of socially-challenged misfits try to go Greek, they're instantly rejected by every house on campus. Deciding to start their own fraternity to protect their outcast brothers, the campus nerds soon find themselves in a battle royale as the Alpha Betas try to crush their new rivals.

programme.title: Back to School
programme.desc: Millionaire businessman Thornton Melon is upset when his son Jason announces that he is not sure about going to college. Thornton insists that college is the best thing he never had for himself, and to prove his point, he agrees to enroll in school along with his son. Thornton is a big hit on campus: always throwing the biggest parties, knowing all the right people, but is this the way to pass college?

programme.title: Austin Powers in Goldmember
programme.desc: The world's most shagadelic spy continues his fight against Dr. Evil. This time, the diabolical doctor and his clone, Mini-Me, team up with a new foe—'70s kingpin Goldmember. While pursuing the team of villains to stop them from world domination, Austin gets help from his dad and an old girlfriend.

programme.title: Happy Gilmore
programme.desc: A Hockey player wannabe finds out that he has the most powerful golf drive in history. He joins the P.G.A. tour to make some money to save grandma's house. The downside is that his hockey player mentality doesn't really go on the P.G.A. tour. Especially with the favorite to win the championship.i

programme.title: The Toy
programme.desc: On one of his bratty son Eric's annual visits, the plutocrat U.S. Bates takes him to his department store and offers him anything in it as a gift. Eric chooses a black janitor who has made him laugh with his antics. At first the man suffers many indignities as Eric's "toy," but gradually teaches the lonely boy what it is like to have and to be a friend.

programme.title: Austin Powers: The Spy Who Shagged Me
programme.desc: When diabolical genius Dr. Evil travels back in time to steal superspy Austin Powers's ‘mojo,’ Austin must return to the swingin' '60s himself - with the help of American agent, Felicity Shagwell - to stop the dastardly plan. Once there, Austin faces off against Dr. Evil's army of minions to try to save the world in his own unbelievably groovy way.

programme.title: Monty Python and the Holy Grail 1974
programme.desc: King Arthur, accompanied by his squire, recruits his Knights of the Round Table, including Sir Bedevere the Wise, Sir Lancelot the Brave, Sir Robin the Not-Quite-So-Brave-As-Sir-Lancelot and Sir Galahad the Pure. On the way, Arthur battles the Black Knight who, despite having had all his limbs chopped off, insists he can still fight. They reach Camelot, but Arthur decides not to enter, as "it is a silly place".

programme.title: Idiocracy
programme.desc: Corporal Joe Bauers, a decidedly average American, is selected for a top-secret hibernation program but is forgotten and left to awaken to a future so incredibly moronic that he's easily the most intelligent person alive.

programme.title: Young Frankenstein
programme.desc: An American grandson of the infamous scientist, struggling to prove that his grandfather was not as insane as people believe, is invited to Transylvania, where he discovers the process that reanimates a dead body.

programme.title: Werewolf of London
programme.desc: While on a botanical expedition in Tibet Dr. Wilfred Glendon is attacked in the dark by a strange animal. Returning to London, he finds himself turning nightly into a werewolf and terrorizing the city, with the only hope for curing his affliction a rare Asian flower, the Mariphasa.

programme.title: Young Frankenstein
programme.desc: An American grandson of the infamous scientist, struggling to prove that his grandfather was not as insane as people believe, is invited to Transylvania, where he discovers the process that reanimates a dead body.

programme.title: The Old Dark House
programme.desc: Seeking shelter from a storm, five travelers are in for a bizarre and terrifying night when they stumble upon the Femm family estate.

programme.title: Killer Klowns from Outer Space
programme.desc: When a small town is invaded by aliens from outer space who are capturing and killing the townspeople, no one takes them seriously. Why? The aliens all look like circus clowns, use weapons that look clown like, and all have painted on smiles. Only a few of the young people in the town realize the danger and of course no one believes them. Armed with an ice cream truck they try and rescue their friends.

programme.title: A Bucket of Blood
programme.desc: A dimwitted busboy at a beatnik cafe passes off a cat he accidentally killed and covered in plaster as a sculpture, prompting a demand for more art that compels him to commit murders.

programme.title: Bud Abbott Lou Costello Meet the Killer Boris Karloff
programme.desc: Lost Caverns Hotel bellhop Freddie Phillips is suspected of murder. Swami Talpur tries to hypnotize Freddie into confessing, but Freddie is too stupid for the plot to work. Inspector Wellman uses Freddie to get the killer (and it isn't the Swami).

programme.title: Abbott and Costello Meet Dr. Jekyll and Mr. Hyde
programme.desc: Slim and Tubby are American cops in London to study police tactics. They wind up in jail and are bailed out by Dr. Jekyll. Jekyll has been murdering fellow doctors who laugh at his experiments. He has more murders in mind. At one point the serum that turns Jekyll into the murderous Hyde gets injected into Tubby.

programme.title: Abbott and Costello Meet Frankenstein
programme.desc: The Wolf Man tries to warn a dimwitted porter that Dracula wants his brain for Frankenstein monster's body.

programme.title: Abbott and Costello Meet the Mummy
programme.desc: In Egypt Peter and Freddie find the archaeologist Dr. Zoomer murdered before they can return to America. A medallion leads them to a crypt where a revived mummy provides the terror.

programme.title: Hold That Ghost
programme.desc: Two bumbling service station attendants are left as the sole beneficiaries in a gangster's will. Their trip to claim their fortune is sidetracked when they are stranded in a haunted house along with several other strangers.

programme.title: Killers from Space
programme.desc: Atomic scientist/pilot Doug Martin is missing after his plane crashes on an reconnaissance mission after a nuclear test. Miraculously appearing unhurt at the base later, he is given sodium amethol, but authorities are skeptical of his story that he was captured by aliens determined to conquer the Earth with giant monsters and insects. Martin vows to use existing technology to destroy them.

programme.title: Halloween III: Season of the Witch
programme.desc: After a terrified toy salesman is mysteriously attacked and brought to the hospital, clutching the year's most popular Halloween mask, Dr. Daniel Challis sets out to investigate the strange events and finds himself thrust into a nightmarish conspiracy.

programme.title: Prince of Space
programme.desc: A Group of space men from the planet Krankor who resemble chickens are led by their leader, Phantom to invade Earth. But a strange superhero named Prince of Space (actually a bootblack in disguise) arrives to defeat the spacemen. Although the weapons of the aliens cannot harm the Prince of Space, the Phantom continues to fight, and many plot twists (including capturing a group of elderly scientists) ensue. Watch out for the Giant!

programme.title: Bride of Frankenstein
programme.desc: Baron Henry Frankenstein and his monster both turn out to be alive, not killed as previously believed. Frankenstein wants to get out of the evil-experiment business, but when mad scientist Dr. Pretorius kidnaps his wife, Frankenstein agrees to help him create a new creature, a woman, to be the companion of the monster.

programme.title: Creature from the Black Lagoon
programme.desc: A scientific expedition searching for fossils along the Amazon River discovers a prehistoric Gill-Man in the legendary Black Lagoon. The explorers capture the mysterious creature, but it breaks free. The Gill-Man returns to kidnap the lovely Kay, fiancée of one in the expedition, with whom he has fallen in love.

programme.title: Creepshow
programme.desc: Five grisly tales from a kid's comic book about a murdered father rising from his grave, a bizarre meteor, a vengeful husband, a mysterious crate's occupant, and a plague of cockroaches.

programme.title: Dracula
programme.desc: Transylvanian vampire Count Dracula bends a naive real estate agent to his will, then takes up residence at a London estate where he sleeps in his coffin by day and searches for potential victims by night.

programme.title: Ed Wood
programme.desc: Ambitious but troubled movie director Edward D. Wood Jr. tries his best to fulfill his dreams despite his lack of talent.

programme.title: ACDC - Live at Caste Donington, England (August 17, 1991)
The Best of Ringo Starr and his All Star Band so far
Buck Cherry and Some Cheryl Crow Woodstock 99
Tom Petty and The Heartbreakers - Storytellers (1999)
Led Zeppelin Live Madison Square Garden
The Rolling Stones Live From Rio
The Eagles - Don Kirshner's Rock Concert (1974)
Columbo
programme.desc: Brilliant photographer Paul Galesko plans to murder his domineering wife, Frances, and pin the crime on an ex-convict named Alvin Deschler. His plan is to make it look as if Alvin kidnapped his wife and killed her before picking up the ransom money.

programme.title: Maude
programme.desc: Carol does her best to try to keep Walter in the house as anticipation mounts for Maude's return from a 3-week stay in Boston. For which, following Vivian's lead, Maude too got a face-lift. There is only one problem: Walter doesn't notice the difference! As usual, all hell breaks loose and Walter and Maude have yet another argument.

programme.title: Maude
programme.desc: The lazy furnace repairman, Whitnauer, has been showing an interest in Florida and she doesn't quite know what to do about his advances. This situation ultimately causes marital problems between Florida and her husband, Henry, when he catches her dancing with the man. Meanwhile, Maude and Walter are finding it hard to get Whitnauer to hurry it up on the furnace.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: The girls' search for a housekeeper ends when they hire Marguerite, a woman who appears to have mystical powers. However, when they fire her, a string of bizarre events convince the girls that Marguerite has placed a curse on them.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.sub-title: Nothing To Fear But Fear Itself
programme.desc: When Rose must give a eulogy at her aunt's funeral, Rose must try to get over her biggest fear in the world, giving speeches. This also brings up Dorothy and Blanche's biggest fears, Dorothy's of flying and Blanche's of a recurring dream.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: Rose becomes concerned about nuclear war when girls in her cadet troop draw pictures of a bleak future. This prompts her to write letters to Gorbachev and Reagan. She's stunned when she gets a response and learns that Gorbechev would like to meet her.

programme.title: The Golden Palace
programme.sub-title: Camp Town Races Aren't Nearly as Much Fun as They Used to Be
programme.desc: When The Daughters of the Traditional South are set to arrive at the hotel, Blanche hangs a Confederate flag on the front counter, though this doesn't bode well with Roland. Meanwhile, Rose is outraged when a married man whom she knows checks in with his mistress.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Fred and Lamont take a tour through the NBC studios and Fred gets a kick out of seeing Johnny Carson's parking spot. However, what Fred really gets a kick out of is seeing Lena Horne's car and sneaks into her dressing room. After meeting her, he tries to trick her into visiting him at his home.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Lamont goes totally African. He starts to wear African clothing, and yearns to learn more about the culture. At the same time, Fred thinks that the whole thing is ridiculous, that is until a woman from Nigeria visits and explains to Lamont how sacred listening to your father is in African culture and how you should respect him.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: When Kelsey finally decides to sell his bar, nothing will stop Archie from getting it--even if it means forging Edith's signature against her will.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: When Cousin Liz dies, Edith and Archie learn more about her than they ever thought. When roomate of Liz, Veronica, comes out of the closet to Edith, she also admits that she was more than just roomates with Liz.

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.sub-title: Connie & Vicki vs. Gabrielle & Tami vs. Debi & Dan

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.sub-title: Barbara & Linda vs. Susan & Karen vs. Ema & Vic

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges are three small time actors looking for a job. They meet three girl dancers in the situation and get a small part in a big producers show at the shipyard. When the rest of the cast doesn't show up, the stooges and the girls must put on the whole show themselves. The show is a hit and the stooges marry the girls and head to Niagara Falls for their honeymoon.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: Defense workers Moe, Larry, and Curly are home to take a nice rest before going back into work. After Curly hurts his tooth on a bone, Moe and Larry try desperately to pull the problem out.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The Stooges hide out from the cops in Indian disguises.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: Joe wins a contest and is promptly fleeced out of his winnings by some con men. When the stooges go to recover his money, the bad guys convince them that they can get rich by posing as children and becoming the wards of a millionaire. The boys go along with the plan, not realizing that the "millionaire" and his pretty niece are in on the scam and are planning to knock them off. The stooges foil the plan and recover Joe's money.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges are stage hands who also have small parts in a big play. They quickly get on the bad side of the producer. First they fail to prevent a famous critic from sneaking into the audience. Then Shemp accidentally adds a pot holder into a cake they bake as a prop. During the play the stooges (as southern gentlemen) and the rest of the cast spit up feathers during what was supposed to be a serious scene. The critic thinks it's a hilarious satire and the boys are redeemed.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The Stooges are entertainers and waiters in a western saloon with the three girls they hope to marry. They become gold miners to pay off their girlfriend's debts, only to find that when they hit the jackpot, the money was robbed from the bank by their boss.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: After the Stooges get in trouble with a cop and run into a building; they're confronted by two convict conmen who offer them a job selling duck hunting club memberships. The boys end up selling memberships to the chief-of-police, mayor, and members of the police force. The pond they all go hunting in doesn't have any ducks and the stooges have to come up with schemes to get out of trouble with the disgruntled police duckhunters.

programme.title: Svengoolie
programme.desc: In the early 20th century a village experienced a series of inexplicable murders. All the victims were young men who had been turned to stone. The perpetrator of these deaths was a being so repulsive that she transformed the onlooker using the power of her deadly stare. Much of the time the creature took the form of a beautiful and seductive woman, but during periods of the full moon she becomes a living horror, vicious and deadly. A professor has come to investigate the deaths, bringing with him his beautiful assistant whose knowledge of the Gorgon is more intimate than anyone would ever realise.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: The Conners risk getting thrown out of a Vegas nightclub, and visit a wedding chapel with Arnie and Nancy.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Becky gets into hot water for helping Darlene with a school assignment; trying to stop smoking cold-turkey has Roseanne frazzled, as does working two jobs.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Thanksgiving brings four generations together to cope with a surprise announcement from Bev.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne and Jackie go to Kansas City to visit their father's mistress; Dan intends to beat up Becky's obnoxious boss.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne talks Leon into letting her play Santa Claus at the mall -- and hears something she wishes she hadn't from one of the kid's mothers, who turns out to be Darlene's new friend and confidante.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: It is Crystal's choice to choose where the gals' night out will be. Crystal chooses bingo and they all hate it. After Roseanne wins $50 playing bingo, she gets hooked on it. Becky and Darlene babysit Little Ed, but when Dan comes home its D.J. and Dan that end up taking care of the baby. Roseanne and Crystal get into a fight, but at the end they make up and Roseanne learns that Crystal is pregnant again.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: When Dan and Arnie struggle to bring their bowling team out of last place, some unexpected competition arises. Meanwhile, Roseanne feels shut out of Jackie's new friendship with Nancy.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: After Roseanne throws her back out, Bev arrives to help out -- and to spoil her grandchildren.

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: In order to attract sales, Grace Brothers decides to create their own radio show, using a story of Mr. Harmond's. With weird sound effects and the department staff as the characters, it's sure to be a hoot.

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Bad news. Grace Brothers may be bought by the Japanese. The staff take their problems to Number Ten, where they speak with President Reagan by phone. Mrs. Thatcher gets fashion tips from Mr. Humphries.

programme.title: Toon in With Me
programme.desc:
Bill shares some big cartoon names with Toony.
Featured cartoons: The Three Little Pups (1953) Dough Ray Me-Ow (1948) Tot Watchers (1958) Robin Goodhood (1970) Catty Cornered (1953) A Taste of Catnip (1966)

programme.title: Columbo
programme.desc: An old lady who writes popular mystery novels strongly suspects that her niece's husband murdered her, so she exacts revenge by asphyxiating him in her house safe while devising a solid alibi. It's up to Columbo to unravel the subterfuge.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline goes into a tailspin and brings Max into the mix when Nicolas tells her he's going to leave his wife for her.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline tries to fit in with Max and the diner gang as they go out to get wild and crazy at the St. Patrick's Day parade, but finds herself longing to practice her former holiday traditions at the Plaza Hotel.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Deke invites Max and Caroline to join him and his parents for dinner at their home, Max is tongue-tied until his mother confides in her about her secret past.

programme.title: WWF Prime Time Wrestling
programme.desc: Gorilla Monsoon and Bobby "The Brain" Heenan host an extraordinary lineup of in-ring action, including Greg "The Hammer" Valentine going one-on-one with Jim "The Anvil" Neidhart. Plus, One Man Gang battles "Hacksaw" Jim Duggan in singles competition, a look at the alliance between "Macho Man" Randy Savage and Zeus, and much more.

programme.title: Bob's Burgers
programme.desc: The Belcher family road-trips down to Florida to visit Linda's parents who live in a senior home that - to Linda's complete horror - is full of senior swingers! Plus, to keep themselves entertained, Tina, Gene and Louise go on a hunt for a mysterious python that allegedly ate one of the residents' dogs.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray fears that he is going to come down with something dreadful after someone sneezes on him. When Marie hears that Ray is under the weather, she doesn't waste any time in coddling her younger son with homemade chicken soup, special treats, and intense TLC, and he laps up every second of it, much to Debra's disgust.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray buys Debra an elaborate Christmas gift in the hopes that she will be so impressed, she will let him go on a weekend golf trip with his buddies. Ray believes he has the whole scenario worked out. However, he doesn't figure on the even more extravagant gift that Debra has purchased for him.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Doug, Carrie and Arthur head out to the airport to visit with Doug's parents while they have a short layover at Kennedy Airport on their way to Montreal. While there, his parents ask him to sign their living will, making him the one responsible for pulling the plug. When his sister Stephanie gets wind of this, she is offended that she wasn't the one asked to be responsible.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: A hurricane devastates the adjoining backyards of the Heffernans and the Sackskbys. When Carrie rejects their idea of combining the two backyards and builds a tall fence, the Sacksbys retaliate by installing an above-ground swimming pool.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: Elaine meets Kevin's friends and feels likes she's entered the world of Bizarro Jerry where her world is in reverse. Jerry has a date with Gillian, a woman with man hands. George hits on a new sob story to try and get in with a beautiful receptionist. Kramer gets an unpaid job at a upscale firm.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: Elaine's staff loses respect for her after seeing her dance; George benefits socially when Elaine labels him a "bad seed"; Kramer's friend forces Jerry into crime.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne lands a job at a chicken restaurant, with a total dweeb for a supervisor. He wants her to work weekends, which she is unable to do, so she invites the boy to dinner and gets Dan to help him assemble his carburetor for school. But will the kindness pay off in the end?

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: With the adults away, Becky and her friend Dana, decide to really live it up. Using the alcoholic beverages in the house, Becky creates two "tornados," and the girls experience next-day hangovers, which can be the side effects from taking that first sip of a near-fatal glass of beer.

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: Dwight was very excited because he got to take Ryan on his first sales call. He couldn't wait to show Ryan how the sales are really done. Before that, the men took a little side trip to the Schrute family beet farm. Dwight thought it would be funny to leave Ryan on the farm.

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: Michael was educating the entire staff about Diwali, the Hindu holiday that Kelly celebrated. Kelly had invited everybody from work to the celebration. Pam was unsure if she was going to make it, she later confided in Angela that she was saddened because she had nobody to attend the celebration with.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer turns to religion to help him get what he wants and is soon awarded the church in an injury settlement. He then turns the church into his personal party pad and Reverend Lovejoy leaves Springfield to wallow in its hedonism.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart learns that he was once a child actor in commercials and that all the money earned was spent by Homer. In retaliation, Bart petitions the court to have himself legally emancipated from his parents and moves out of the Simpson house. Bart is initially miserable in his new apartment, but discovers that skateboarder Tony Hawk lives on one of the floors above. He and Hawk become friends, and Bart is content with his new life. Homer apologizes to Bart and requests that he return home. Bart accepts the apology, but tells his family that he is going on tour with Hawk. At an event, Homer speaks to Bart and finally promises Bart that he will never treat him badly again.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When challenged to think up a new Christmas carol, Homer instead writes a song about how much he hates Ned Flanders. The song becomes such a hit that the Simpsons become sick of hearing it and take a trip to a dude ranch. Lisa develops affections for a local boy named Luke but becomes jealous when he hears him talking to a girl named Clara on the telephone. One night, she meets Clara and gives her the wrong directions, leading her to become lost. After Lisa discovers that Clara is actually Luke's sister, she rushes to find her. She saves Clara, and decides to tell Luke what happened, but he is offended and dumps her. Meanwhile, Bart and Homer decide to help a tribe of Native Americans who want a beaver dam removed so they can reclaim their land.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer throws out Santa's Little Helper for not saving him from a fire, but soon the dog becomes Duff's new mascot.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Because of Homer losing his driver's license, Marge has to drive everyone everywhere, which is so stressful for her that she accidentally runs over Homer leading to the two seeing a marriage counselor.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: An insurance agent is assigned to make sure Al is afraid to wear shoes to corroborate his lawsuit against the mall that claims he is afraid of shoes after being tied up by a burglar while at work. But Al must decide between the money from the settlement or winning a bowling championship when he's not allowed to bowl barefoot.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Kelly's new secret admirer turns out to be a wealthy, spoiled, immature 12-year-old boy named Robby Bennett, who blackmails her into accompanying him to his middle-school junior prom. Bud also crashes the prom disguised as a music DJ to taunt Kelly until she finally cries out for help, and Bud saves her by contacting Robby's father--who eyes Kelly too.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.sub-title: Guess Who's Coming to Breakfast, Lunch and Dinner
programme.desc: Al is excited when Bud decides to move out, but a bit disappointed when Bud moves back in to the basement, and even more horrified when Peg's mother comes to live with them after splitting up with Peg's father.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.sub-title: A Shoe Room with a View
programme.desc: Al and Griff convinces Gary to use the extra space for an aerobic's studio in the hopes that it'll attract pretty women. When it attracts fat women instead, Kelly convinces her father's boss to hire Ramon. It turns the tide against Feminist Marcy.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: After the Bundy family dog, Buck, dies, he goes to Animal Heaven where his fate for eternity is to be dealt with by a feline judge. Back on Earth, Al tries to cheer up the mourning Kelly by buying her another pet in the form of a parakeet she likes it and names it Tweety, until Peggy's Mom eats it. Al then hires Father Guido Sarducci to conduct a séance to try to contact Buck in the afterlife.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Al and his men's group NO MA'AM decide to form their own church in order to avoid paying beer tax. When Marcy and her women's group FANG expose Al's plan to the public, NO MA'AM surprisingly receives sympathy and donations from thousands of long-suffering married men in America which makes it possible for them to open their church. Al becomes a televangelist and preaches the NO MA'AM way of life.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: While researching for her new part of playing a scientist, Kelly discovers the new color bleen. Al finds that bleen grows hair.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Peggy takes up a self-defense class. What's worse, she is soon promoted to the advanced class. This makes Al feel emasculated. To make Peg drop the self-defense class, Kelly suggests Al take Peg out on a romantic date to see the Director's Cut of The Bridges of Madison Country.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Fred is sure that his dreams will be good luck in picking numbers for a lottery and his hunch turns out to be true, despite the fact that Lamont forbids him to participate. Fred quickly finds himself $500 richer but also finds Aunt Ethel and Bubba needing quick cash.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: After getting in an accident with the truck, Fred thinks that he might have whiplash and Fred's friend Bubba convinces him that he can make big money off of the situation. Lamont is against the whole idea but Fred insists on going through with a lawsuit, but first Fred must identify the person the hit him.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Fred's cousin arrives with his new wife and his not-so-attractive stepdaughter, Betty Jean. When Fred finds out that she will inherit $10,000 if she marries, he tries to fix it so that Lamont will marry her. However, Fred's plan backfires when Lamont catches on to his scheme.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Donna, Fred's fiancee, brings a patient, Osgood Wilcox over for dinner at the Sanford residence and it becomes apparent that Osgood is interested in more than just medical care from Donna. Meanwhile, Lamont encourages Fred to believe there is something going on between Osgood and Donna resulting in a confrontation.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Fred's toothache becomes an exercise in patience for Lamont when Fred refuses to see a dentist and instead tries Bubba's home remedy and a hypnotist. Finally, Fred decides to throw in the towel and visits a dentist but insists on a white dentist.

programme.title: Get a Life
programme.desc: Chris becomes a construction worker with the men who are renovating his parents kitchen. When they tell him one of their trade secrets, Chris has a problem with their dishonesty.

programme.title: Get a Life
programme.desc: Chris loses his wallet in the big city, and the big city give him the key to the city and other perks to make him feel better.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Sylvester Cat, Tweety Bird, and a bulldog are passengers on a train. Sylvester's attempts to catch Tweety are thwarted by the bulldog and a conscientious conductor.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Two sworn enemies mistake Bugs for the enemy.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Yosemite Sam, as Sam von Schpamm the Hessian, attacks Bugs Bunny's fort during the Battle of Bagel Heights in the American War of Independence.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Sylvester Cat spots Tweety Bird in a San Fransisco apartment and tries to gain access but cannot make it past Granny or the cat-hating desk clerk.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: On Be Kind to Animals Week, Porky Pig decides to practice the principle and affectionately pets a large, slobbering dog. The dog takes an instant liking to Porky and follows the pig everywhere.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Bugs Bunny gives Elmer Fudd a close shave as they sing and act out Rossini's opera, The Barber of Seville.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Sylvester Cat finds that his owner have gone on vacation and left him with a large stash of canned food. A pesky mouse torments him with the only can opener.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Tweety Bird is shoveling out his nest atop a city pole after a snowstorm and is spotted by Sylvester Cat and a one-eyed orange tabby, who fight over Tweety.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head Do the Universe
programme.desc: After a "creative" judge sentences them to space camp, a black hole sends our adolescent heroes 24 years into the modern future where the duo misuse iPhones, embark on a quest to score, and become targets of the Deep State.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head try to score with a vice cop who is working undercover as a prostitute.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc:
Beavis and Butt-head visit a construction site; Butt-head gets stuck in a pipe and his rescue becomes a media event. Then, of course, Beavis gets stuck in it.
videos:
Salt 'N Pepa featuring En Vogue - "What A Man"
Mark O'Connor - "The Devil Comes Back To Georgia"
Pantera - "I'm Broken"

programme.title: Mike Judge's Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Butt-Head is given medication for his aggression towards Beavis and becomes a little too nice.

programme.title: Pee-wee's Playhouse
programme.desc: Pee-wee's revving up the presses to print the Playhouse's first newspaper! The Playhouse crew starts researching their articles with the five "W's" and "H": who, what, when, where, why, and "how." When Pee-wee puts the paper to bed, and then goes to bed himself, sneaky Randy changes everyone's stories to lies! How will the Playhouse pals live it down?

programme.title: Pee-wee's Playhouse
programme.desc: Mrs. Rene is learning how to tango! With a rose in her mouth and Pee-wee's magic footprints on the floor, she dances up a storm until Pee-wee dips her right out the Playhouse door! Cowboy Curtis remembers his first and last whirl on rollerskates, and Jambi helps Pee-wee relive the whole show with a replay that goes by all too "fast."

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.sub-title: And The Wedding Cake Cake Cake
programme.desc: A girl who likes Max's cupcakes hires Max to make her wedding cake. Max who is still reeling from her break up, is hesitant to do it. She does it but the bride is constantly changing her mind about the cake and her marriage.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: Tony's gambling spiral strains his finances and his relationships with Carmela and Hesh, while A.J. faces a big step with Blanca and Vito's widow asks Tony for help with her increasingly troubled son.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: As A.J. sinks deeper into depression after his breakup, Tony and Carmela clash over how to help him, while a business slight involving Christopher's father-in-law sparks new tension between Christopher and Paulie.

programme.title: The Good Place
programme.desc: Michael works with the Joint Council of Afterlife Affairs to smooth out kinks in the system. Jason and Tahani move on. Eleanor tries to influence Chidi.

programme.title: House of the Dragon
programme.desc: Princess Rhaenyra dives deep into the prophecies about House Targaryen while confronting a realm that resists the idea of a woman on the Iron Throne, and Prince Daemon, embittered over being passed over as the heir to Westeros, prepares to go to war.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: House Frey joins forces with House Tully. Jon faces his most difficult test yet.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: Joffrey challenges Tywin. Dany waits to see if she is a conqueror or a liberator.

programme.title: House
programme.desc: Cuddy wants House to diagnose a psychiatrist who collapse at the South Pole. And she can't leave the station so House has to diagnose her via video link and she doesn't let House walk all over her, if anything she intrigues him. House is also frustrated that there's no longer any cable service and tries to get it restored. And when Wilson starts sneaking around and is being secretive House wonders why.

programme.title: House
programme.desc: A former music producer who had recently---and suddenly---converted to Hasidic Judaism collapses at her wedding, and House suspects that the reasons behind her sudden conversion might also have something to do with her mysterious illness. Meanwhile, Wilson is dating a woman whose personality is a lot like House's.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa tells Bart that toilet water swirls in the opposite direction in the Southern Hemisphere. Determined to find out if this is true, Bart makes a collect call to Australia. When the father of the boy he called finds out about the charges, it sparks an international incident. The Simpsons must travel to Australia to repair relations with the United States.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: After a quarrel with Dr. Hibbert, Homer decides to enlist Maggie for a pre-nursery school. Maggie doesn't get tested because she can't talk. Back home Lisa discovers Maggie can make words and the family goes back to the pre-nursery school. This time Maggie passes the test gloriously and turns out to have an IQ of 167. This is more than Lisa's IQ (159) which makes her unsure about her identity.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: An irreverent take on the Prince and the Pauper. Bart enthusiastically trades lives with a wealthy lookalike after landing himself in trouble for triggering a rampage of vacuum cleaners, but finds the lavish lifestyle too much when he becomes the target of all his new friends' pranks.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer decides not to attend church one Sunday and has the time of his life and vows never to return to church again, however one Sunday after falling asleep while smoking a cigar he accidentally sets fire to The Simpsons house, Flanders spots the flames and rescues him, Homer comes to his senses and turns up at church the following Sunday(asleep).

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa's ability to pick winning football teams kicks off a strong bond with Homer.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Krusty's Hollywood pals help when his show is canceled after a new star causes his ratings to plummet.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Reverend Lovejoy subjects the congregation to an extended Bible reading, the Simpsons fall asleep and have dreams based on Bible stories.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer discovers that he has an illegitimate half-brother who is now the very successful car manufacturer Herb Powell. The Simpsons are invited to stay at Herb's mansion and, considering Homer to be an average American and typical consumer, Herb asks him to design a new car for the company. It is, however, a total disaster and results in Herb's bankruptcy.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Whilst at the carnival Homer and Bart accidentally put two of its workers, Cooder and his son Spud, out of business, and invite them to stay at the house. However, the couple lure the Simpsons away with the promise of a free boat ride and take over the house, changing the locks. Homer challenges Cooder to a little game, the winner of which takes possession of the house - but of course Homer cheats to obtain his victory.

programme.title: WWE Raw
programme.desc: As "Stone Cold" Steve Austin looks for payback on The Undertaker and Paul Bearer, the he finds an unlikely ally. When X-Pac calls out Commissioner Shawn Michaels for siding with The Corporation, The Heartbreak Kid forces his former friend to defend his European Championship against Ken Shamrock. Plus, Mankind, The Rock, Al Snow, and more in action.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Johnny and Stevie celebrate having a fully booked hotel; David and Patrick go on a date.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: The once opulently rich Roses, following news that their now absent business manager has left them financially ruined, are left by the government with only a modest income and one asset, Schitt's Creek.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline persuades Max to take a cupcake-decorating class.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: The two take a job cleaning a hoarder's apartment for the extra money; Max learns that Johnny has a secret.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Johnny's girlfriend has a devious plan in mind when she hires Max and Caroline to bake cupcakes for an art show featuring his work.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline and Max spend Caroline's first broke holiday season working at a department store's Santaland to earn extra money.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline try to find Chestnut a job in order to get him lodging for the winter. Meanwhile, Peach, the mother of the twins Max babysits, auditions for "Real Housewives of TriBeCa".

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Max's stove breaks, Caroline tries to return some expensive rings to Barney's department store, but discovers her surname is no longer an asset at her former home away from home.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline discovers the secret ingredient to Max's cupcakes whilst in the midst of her new obsession couponing.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline's new neighbor draws their suspicion.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline try to prove their cleaning skills to Sophie so she will hire them for her maid service.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Sophie's (Jennifer Coolidge) beauty overwhelms Earl, Caroline and Max wind up spending Valentine's Day at the hospital; Caroline tries to rekindle an old flame.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline have to make kosher cupcakes when they get a gig at a bar mitzvah.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max runs into an old boyfriend, when she accompanies Caroline to see her father in jail.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline and Max spend their spring break dog-sitting at a couple's lavish apartment.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max convinces Caroline to partake in a clinical drug trial in an effort to raise money to help Caroline’s father.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: After promising Earl to drop his tax return in the mail, Caroline learns that Max has never filed, and gets sidetracked trying to help her.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: The stakes are high when a socialite Manhattan mother hires Max and Caroline to serve cupcakes at her daughter's first birthday party.

programme.title: Ted Lasso
programme.desc: After the semifinal, Beard sets out on an all-night odyssey through London in an effort to collect his thoughts.

programme.title: Ted Lasso
programme.desc: Rebecca is stunned by a sudden loss. The team rallies to show their support, but Ted finds himself grappling with a piece of his past.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman explains how researchers use the scientific method to figure things out. Josie and Lester help find out if salt water conducts electricity.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman is asked about vaccinations, and calls upon Dr. Edward Jenner to explain how he came up with the idea. He later does a truck with water and a funnel and examines heat from friction.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman explains thermodynamics by showing how hot things cool and cold things warm up to room temperature, and how molecules move faster when they are hot than when they are cold. Afterward, Beakman explains why people get pimples.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman describes fossils and why they are important clues to learn what the world was like thousands of years ago. Dr. Louis Leakey explains that fossils are formed when plant or animal remains are quickly buried in sediment.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman demonstrates how people breath using a model of the diaphragm and lungs. Beakman also explains the sending of signals via telephones.

programme.title: Bill Nye: The Science Guy
programme.desc: Water is massive; rivers are powerful. As rivers flow downhill, they wear away rock and soil to form canyons or winding curves in the land, called meanders. Sometimes rivers fill and overflow their banks. Rivers with too much water create floods that can carry away plants, trees, buildings and boulders. Rivers and streams support most of the ecosystems on land.

programme.title: Bill Nye: The Science Guy
programme.desc: All food, whether it’s protein, fat, carbohydrates, vitamins, or minerals, is made of chemicals. When your body gets a hold of these chemicals, it recombines them and makes energy. Different types of food make different amounts of energy, which are measured in calories. How do scientists figure out the amount of calories in food? In this episode, Bill will reveal the secrets of the bomb calorimeter – an instrument of food science.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Rob gets new powers through a remote control and tries to destroy Gumball's world.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Fed up with having to listen to Molly's long, tedious anecdotes, Gumball and Darwin try to teach her how to make them sound more exciting. When this doesn't work, they decide their only option is to give Molly a story worth telling.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: In this sequel to last season's "The Disaster", Gumball travels back to the events of the previous episode to prevent Rob from ruining his life, but everything he does makes things worse.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball and Darwin are wary of Anais' new friend, Josh, making her question his motives as well.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball and Darwin search for adventure during a walk around Elmore.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: After finding a secret manifesto on Alan's memory stick, Gumball and Darwin decide to foil his plan for world domination.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Nicole reminisces about how she met Richard during a disastrous family dinner, and how things could have turned out for her if she had not.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: It turns out the Wattersons have been using the Robinsons' WiFi, but when Mr. Robinson changes the password, the Watterson household is left without internet and Gumball and Darwin attempt to recreate it.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: When he is labeled a "loser" in an online test, Gumball believes he must do the opposite of everything he would normally do. Meanwhile, Darwin and Sarah notice Tobias is stealing Gumball's life and role in the series in the form of a cheesy sitcom.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Rocky decides to stop living life online, and find love on a dating app, and Gumball and Darwin try to help him.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball and Darwin give Bobert instructions so he will behave in a safe and responsible manner, but he applies his logic to their rules.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Beast Boy is tired of being the least intelligent of the Titans. When all of his efforts to get smart fail, he uses Raven's spell book to cast a spell - not to make him smarter, but to make all of the other Titans dumber.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Robin infiltrates the H.I.V.E. Tower to destroy it, but instead he is distracted by its awesomeness!

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Tired of being the only one of the Titans without a little buddy sidekick, Cyborg adopts Brother Blood's henchman, PainBot, and sneaks him into the tower. Now Cyborg just has to keep PainBot from harming the other Titans.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Killer Moth offers a reward for Silkie's return, but will Silkie be happier away from the Titans?

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Hoping to learn how to be actually funny - unlike Robin who can only tell "uncle jokes" - Starfire asks to join Cyborg and Beast Boy's twosome. Robin is upset as he feels this throws off the balance of the Tower and goes to the extreme to deal with it.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Robin tries to teach Spanish-speaking brothers his superhero ways.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The dream worlds of the Titans.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Cyborg is possessed by a "Grandma Voice", which irritates the Titans.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Robin dabbles with magic, ignoring Raven's warning that he will anger the magic god.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Robin makes marionette versions of the other Titans as a way to deal with their lack of respect. However, he takes things a little too far when he wishes the real Titans were more like his puppets and The Puppet Wizard grants his wish.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren gets driven out of his mind yet again as his anxiously awaited cousin Svën forms a stupidity bond with Stimpy through magic Nose Goblins, Kitty Litter, and the "Don't Whiz on the Electric Fence" board game.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren and Stimpy visit a haunted mansion where a depressive ghost fruitlessly tries to scare them, but the clueless duo barely notice his antic... not even the chainsaw!

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren & Stimpy, starving, exploit the benefits of living as babies. Then, George Liquor American takes us the Dog Show, where Ren & Stimpy are pitted against some tough competition. But only George can handle the judge's harsh scrutiny.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Stimpy squeezes, and a stench is born. Ren thinks it's just a stinky fantasy, but when Stinky floats away, Stimpy abandons his friend to search through rain and snow and dark of night. It'll take a Christmas miracle for Stinky to return to his warm, snugly home!

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren and Stimpy want the easy life of living in a zoo. They disguise themselves as monkeys and try to hang in there.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head deal with annoying customers (and deep fry stuff) during a shift at Burger World.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head go to the doctor to get prescription medicine, so they can "feel good."

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Butthole and Joe paint Mr. Anderson's house. Bored with that, they sniff paint thinner, destroy quite a few things, paint his cat's butt, and decide to go for a nice ride on his lawnmower.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head head to Mexico with their friend, Dave, in an effort to obtain illegal fireworks, but instead make a run for the bathroom after a taco stand owner gets revenge on the duo who fed his dog hot sauce.

programme.title: Double Dare
programme.sub-title: Dynamite Dudes (Ken & Alissa) vs. Gold Canaries (Tyra & Amy)

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Mr. Wizard Explains the Universe will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Nerdy Matthew annoys the entire cast.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Mr. T Goes to a Tupperware Party will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
The cast gets Ross to throw them a party. What Ross does, however, is have a cardboard cake and water.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Garbage of the Rich & Famous will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
After local garbage men go on strike, the studio becomes a storage area for the city's garbage. Christine is carried out of the studio in a garbage bag.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
The Commercial Messages will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
In the sketches, the cast does many parodies of TV commercials. They also try to sell some of Ross' malfunctioning products as part of another get-rich-quick scheme.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Walton's Mountain Becomes an Open Pit Mine will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
The cast gets back to nature and make up the set like a farm.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Lassie Flunks Obedience School will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Ross decided to tutor the cast. He covers everything from reading to advanced chemistry.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
St. Elsewhere will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Everyone who has green slime dumped on them comes down with a mysterious flu.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Hulk Hogan vs. Punkey Brewster in Mismatch of the Century will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Ross gets the cast ready for the arrival of the cast of the Russian version of YCDTOTV.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
J.R. Ewing Saves His Money to Buy a Skateboard will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Alasdair and Adam compete over the new girl in the cast, while at the same time trying to save money for a car.

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
Taking their chances in this episode, the gambling industry takes on the medical professionals. Also, we see how MXC is seen in the foreign nation of New Jersey.
Games include: Sack Lunch; Boulder Dash; Tight Enders; Brass Balls

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
People with hobbies take on food service employees to see who's got what it takes to survive elimination. Kenny discusses his leisure activities, and the Captain shares his opinion on hobbies with the contestants.
Games include: Sinkers and Floaters; Saddle Sores; Wallbangers; Log Drop

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc: Hi-Tech vs. Civil Service. Hi-Tech computer nerds take on the Government Civil Servants. It's a bombastic brawl pitting brilliance vs. bureaucracy, MXC style. Games: Dope on a Rope, Circle Jerkers, Swamp Gassers, and Brass Balls.

programme.title: MXC
programme.sub-title: Cable TV Workers vs. White House Employees
programme.desc:
Finding out who's more ruthless: cable TV workers or White House employees. Meanwhile, Kenny remembers a White House tour.
Games include: Rotating Surfboards of Death; Door Jam; Mine Games; Tumbling Dominoes of Doom

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
Real life and wild life battle it out like animals on this episode of MXC, as reality TV stars face off against animal specialists.
Games include: Window Pain; Dash to Death; Loogie Launch; Endangering Species; Log Drop

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
The battle looms between office workers and toy makers in this MXC battle.
Games include: Holes of Glory; Skidmarkers; Log Drop; Chum in the Mouth

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
Can the military take down the big lacquered hair, perfect bodies and personalities of the beauty pageant contestants?
Games include: Sinkers and Floaters; Turtle Hurdlers Crossing the Poo-Tomac; Pole Riders; Boulder Dash

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
Hoteliers go head-to-head with entrepreneurs in this battle. Captain Tenneal speaks on the dangers of hotel rooms.
Games include: Dirty Muddy Balls; Saddle Sores; Staff Infectors; Rotating Surfboard of Death

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Daria and Quinn arrive at their new school. Quinn makes friends easily. Our Daria does not. We meet Daria's teachers, her family, and her new best friend, Jane.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Grateful for her help in art class, dimwitted cheerleader Brittany Taylor invites Daria to her popular-crowd party in a gated community. Though uninterested, Daria realizes that attending the party would be a perfect opportunity to humiliate Quinn. She takes Jane along for kicks...hopefully.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Fearing that Daria and Quinn won't be prepared for college, after meeting some clients who are preparing their three-year-old daughter for it, Helen and Jake force them into Lawndale High's college prep course, and take them on a tour of their alma mater; Middleton College. Like so many parents, they find college life has changed since they've been there, and both girls get caught up in two different activities while getting the guided tour.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Lawndale's local cybercafé has a break-in, and Mr. O'Neill thinks he can prevent future vandalism and theft by restoring it as one of the hip coffee houses of the 1960's. When Daria is forced into joining the other students into raising money for repairs, and later performing on stage with one of her essays, the kids, parents, and teachers of Lawndale have no idea what she has in store for them.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Mrs. Bennett takes her economics class on a field trip to the Mall of the Millenium. Daria and Jane want nothing to do with this trip, but have no way out of it, however all is not lost when Daria discovers Quinn and the rest of the Fashion Club skipping school there.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Lawndale High has a couple of visitors, claiming to be modeling scouts looking for the next Christy Turlington. Quinn and Brittany have high hopes. Daria and Jane are revolted by their presence.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Daria and Kevin are forced to work as lab-partners in science class. The homework consists of teaching a mouse tricks. Daria does all the work, of course, while Kevin watches football on TV and flirts with Quinn.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Quinn gets Daria to babysit for her. Daria, who has no interest in being an authority figure since she doesn't respect authority, is stuck with two well-behaved "PC" kids.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Someone has stuffed the most important seats in England with cactus. Danger Mouse surmises the pointy plants come from the giant saguaros. Therefore, he and Penfold head for Mexico where they soon home in on Baron Greenback. Unfortunatelly, the villainous toad has an angry bull on his side.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Wufgang Bah, the world's worst composer, has used a ray to destroy all the music on the planet so people will have to listen to his. Dangermouse and Penfold are on the case, using a cassette deck with the last remaining tape of background music, without which they are powerless.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Penfold's hearing problems make him immune to a device that brainwashes anyone who hears Greenback's voice.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: A spaceship has crash landed in Birmingham city center. DM and Penfold meet an alien who is looking for a small furry creature called a Ticklehipos, that just loves to climb up trouser legs and multiply.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.sub-title: The Spy Who Stayed in With a Cold
programme.desc: Agent 57 teams up with Danger Mouse to defeat Baron Greenback when Penfold stays home with a cold; the only problem is 57 gets it too and every time he sneezes he changes disguise.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: A new experimental enzyme being tested at the Wonder White Sub company laboratory bonds with an aggressive molecule and escapes down the drain. It starts attacking people, pinching underwear and tries to turn everything white.

programme.title: Mr. Wizard's World
programme.desc: - Flashpaper - Broom Trick - Panama Canal - Hero's Voice - Nipple Expansion - Exercise For Cows - Reaction Time - Solar Mirrors - Tablecloth Pull

programme.title: Mr. Wizard's World
programme.desc: - Hot Air Balloon - NYC To Tokyo - Whispering Tube - Hero's Hand - Robots In The Factory - Optical Illusions - I-Beam
